title: Unlimited Tools MCP signed profile bundle (E13 design)
description: DESIGN (E13, Gate C of E09): a signed, self-contained distribution envelope for permissioned tool profiles (docs/mcp-signed-profile-bundles.md). A bundle wraps the E09 profile map with issuer, audience, validity window, an upstream-namespace ceiling, an optional revocation pointer, and a MANDATORY detached Ed25519 signature computed over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed. Verification is local against a trusted-keys file (no PKI, no network fetch in v1); every verification failure is fail-closed refuse-all with a reserved code (-32015 bundle_signature_invalid, -32016 bundle_expired, -32017 bundle_revoked, -32018 bundle_audience_mismatch, -32019 bundle_key_missing; malformed bundles reuse -32014 profile_invalid). The embedded 'profiles' map duplicates the E09 shape and constraints from schemas/mcp-tool-profile.schema.json verbatim (self-contained schema, no cross-file $ref, matching repo style); the E09 semantic load checks (extends exists, no self-reference, no cycles, chain depth <= 8, callable covered by visible, default_profile exists) plus the bundle-level checks (issued_at strictly before expires_at, signature.key_id equals issuer.key_id, every profile rule covered by allowed_upstream_namespaces) are enforced by the gateway at load time; this schema enforces the structural format. NOTHING in E13 verifies signatures at runtime; enforcement is E14.

issuer.description: Who issued the bundle. Trust decisions use key_id plus the local trusted-keys file ONLY; display is for audit rows and refusal messages and grants nothing.

key_id.description: Opaque identifier of the signing key; must equal signature.key_id (semantic load check). Never a key value.

display.description: Human-readable issuer name for audit and messages. Never used for trust decisions.

audience.description: Who the bundle was issued FOR: '<scheme>:<name>' identifiers with exactly three schemes (team:, org:, host:). The consumer presents its own identifiers (--audience-id / UNLIMITED_SKILLS_MCP_AUDIENCE); an empty intersection is refused fail-closed with bundle_audience_mismatch (-32018). An empty audience is unrepresentable — there is deliberately no 'anyone' bundle.

issued_at.description: Start of the validity window, RFC 3339 UTC ('Z' only). Must be strictly before expires_at (semantic load check). Outside the window (with 300 s skew tolerance) is refused fail-closed with bundle_expired (-32016).

expires_at.description: End of the validity window, RFC 3339 UTC ('Z' only). Expiry is mandatory — an immortal bundle would make revocation the only freshness mechanism. Recommended validity <= 90 days.

allowed_upstream_namespaces.description: The bundle's upstream ceiling, in the same two-form E09 rule grammar (exact '<upstream>.<tool>' or '<upstream>.*'; no regex, no partial globs, no wildcard upstream segment). Every visible/callable rule in every embedded profile must be covered by this list (semantic load check); an uncovered rule is refused fail-closed with bundle_audience_mismatch (-32018).

default_profile.description: Same meaning as the E09 top-level default_profile, scoped to this bundle's 'profiles' map. Selection precedence is unchanged: --profile > UNLIMITED_SKILLS_MCP_PROFILE > this default; nothing resolved is refused fail-closed with profile_not_found (-32013).

revocation.description: Optional revocation pointer, design-only in v1. crl_path names a local CRL file (revoked bundle SHA-256s and revoked key_ids); a declared-but-unreadable CRL is fail-closed bundle_revoked (-32017) — 'cannot prove not-revoked' never degrades to 'trusted'. registry_endpoint is reserved for the future registry gate and is NEVER fetched in v1.

crl_path.description: Local CRL file path ('~' expansion allowed; relative paths are a load error).

registry_endpoint.description: Reserved https URL for future online revocation. Carried, never dereferenced in v1.

profiles.description: The E09 profile map, embedded verbatim (shape and constraints duplicated from schemas/mcp-tool-profile.schema.json). Bundles are self-contained: 'extends' resolves only within this map; no cross-file parents exist in either direction.

extends.description: Single parent profile in THIS bundle (no multiple inheritance, no cross-file parents). Inheritance is restriction-only: the child's effective sets are intersected with the parent's — a child can narrow but never widen. Self-reference, cycles, dangling names, and chains deeper than 8 are load errors (profile_invalid, -32014).

visible.description: Visibility rules: exact '<upstream>.<tool>' or '<upstream>.*' (whole upstream). Filters tools_search and gates tools_schema; an invisible tool is refused with tool_not_visible (-32011), indistinguishable from a nonexistent one. Absent: a child inherits the parent's set; a root profile denies everything. Every rule must additionally be covered by the bundle's allowed_upstream_namespaces.

callable.description: Callability rules, same grammar; gates tools_call. Always a subset of visible: every rule here must be covered by this profile's visible rules (load check), and evaluation additionally requires visibility. A visible-but-not-callable tool is refused with tool_not_callable (-32012). Absent: a child inherits the parent's set; a root profile denies everything. Every rule must additionally be covered by the bundle's allowed_upstream_namespaces.

signature.description: MANDATORY detached-signature block (a bundle without a signature is not a bundle), same envelope shape as the optional E09 profile-file slot. Computed over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed. A signature that does not verify — or is absent where the signed-required policy demands one — is refused fail-closed with bundle_signature_invalid (-32015); an unknown key_id with bundle_key_missing (-32019).

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. Gate C fixes ed25519; widening the enum requires a new reviewed design.

key_id.description: Identifier of the verification key in the local trusted-keys file; must equal issuer.key_id (semantic load check). Never a key value.
